paper_metadata.title: CTRL: A Conditional Transformer Language Model for Controllable Generation
paper_metadata.abstract: Large-scale language models show promising text generation capabilities, but users cannot easily control particular aspects of the generated text. We release CTRL, a 1.63 billion-parameter conditional transformer language model, trained to condition on control codes that govern style, content, and task-specific behavior. Control codes were derived from structure that naturally co-occurs with raw text, preserving the advantages of unsupervised learning while providing more explicit control over text generation. These codes also allow CTRL to predict which parts of the training data are most likely given a sequence. This provides a potential method for analyzing large amounts of data via model-based source attribution. We have released multiple full-sized, pretrained versions of CTRL at https://github.com/salesforce/ctrl.

extraction_data.innovation: CTRL introduces a conditional transformer language model that uses control codes to govern style, content, and task-specific behavior. These codes are derived from natural co-occurring structures in text (e.g., domains, topics, dates), enabling explicit control over generation while preserving unsupervised learning advantages.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Wikipedia (En, De, Es, Fr), Project Gutenberg, 45 subreddits, OpenWebText, news data (Hermann et al., 2015; Barrault et al., 2019; Sandhaus, 2008; Grusky et al., 2018), Amazon Reviews (McAuley et al., 2015), Europarl and UN data from WMT (En-De, En-Es, En-Fr) (Barrault et al., 2019), question-answer pairs (no context documents) from ELI5 (Fan et al., 2019) and the MRQA shared task (Stanford Question Answering Dataset, NewsQA, TriviaQA, SearchQA, HotpotQA, Natural Questions)
extraction_data.extension: Control codes are prepended as the first token in sequences to condition generation on specific domains or styles, enabling fine-grained control over output content.
extraction_data.application: Text generation, conditional generation, task-specific generation, zero-shot code-mixing
extraction_data.paper_title: CTRL: A Conditional Transformer Language Model for Controllable Generation
